user.title: Connect to an rtl_433 server
user.description: Enter the WebSocket connection details for your rtl_433 HTTP server (started with `-F http`). Use plain `ws://` by default, or enable Secure to use `wss://` through a TLS reverse proxy. No authentication is performed by this integration.

data.manage_settings: Manage rtl_433 settings from Home Assistant

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the rtl_433 server.
data_description.port: TCP port of the rtl_433 HTTP server (default 8433).
data_description.path: WebSocket endpoint path on the server (default /ws).
data_description.secure: Connect over wss:// instead of ws:// (for a TLS reverse proxy).
data_description.manage_settings: When on, Home Assistant adopts this server's current SDR settings and re-applies them on every reconnect, and adds controls for frequency, gain, and more. Turn off to leave the server's own configuration untouched.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure rtl_433 hub
reconfigure.description: Update this hub's connection target. Nested devices and their history are preserved.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the rtl_433 server.
data_description.port: TCP port of the rtl_433 HTTP server (default 8433).
data_description.path: WebSocket endpoint path on the server (default /ws).
data_description.secure: Connect over wss:// instead of ws:// (for a TLS reverse proxy).

hassio_confirm.title: Confirm adding rtl_433 radio
hassio_confirm.description: You are about to add a rtl_433 radio published by the {addon} add-on at `{host}:{port}`.

abort.already_configured: This rtl_433 server is already configured.
abort.invalid_discovery_info: The discovery message was missing required information.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect. Check the host, port, and path, and that the rtl_433 server is reachable.

hub.title: Hub settings
hub.description: Configure new-device discovery and the default availability timeout for this rtl_433 server. rtl_433 connection settings are in the three-dot overflow menu under "reconfigure".

data.manage_settings: Manage rtl_433 settings from Home Assistant

data_description.discovery_enabled: When on, newly observed devices on this server are added automatically as nested devices.
data_description.availability_timeout: Mark a device unavailable after this many seconds without an event. Use 0 to never mark devices unavailable. Devices that send rare events (door, window, motion) automatically get a longer default.
data_description.manage_settings: When on, Home Assistant adopts this server's current SDR settings and re-applies them on every reconnect, and adds controls for frequency, gain, and more. Turn off to leave the server's own configuration untouched.

device.title: Device settings
device.description: Pick a device and override its availability timeout. Leave the timeout blank to clear the override and use the hub default. For a utility meter, choose a commodity to calibrate its consumption sensor for the Energy dashboard.

data_description.device: The device whose settings you want to change.
data_description.timeout_override: Mark this device unavailable after this many seconds without an event. Use 0 to never mark it unavailable. Leave blank to use the automatic default for this device's type (longer for door/window/motion sensors).
data_description.commodity: What this meter measures. Pick Energy, Gas, or Water to calibrate the consumption sensor (next step) so it works in the Energy dashboard. Pick None to leave it unitless (and clear any existing calibration).
data_description.motion_clear_delay: For a motion sensor, auto-clear it to off this many seconds after the last motion. Leave blank to use the default.

mappings.title: Device mappings
mappings.description: Add or correct this hub's device-library mappings as YAML, using the same schema as the shipped library. See [how to write mappings]({docs_url}) for the format, examples, and how to scope an override to one device model. {problems}

calibration.title: Calibrate consumption ({commodity})
calibration.description: Set the base unit one raw consumption count represents and the scale applied to the counter. The sensor reports raw count x scale in this unit; Home Assistant converts to your preferred display unit. Changing this rebuilds the sensor and starts fresh long-term statistics.

data_description.unit: The unit one consumption count represents (before scale). Home Assistant converts between compatible units for display.
data_description.scale: Multiplier applied to the raw counter (for example 0.1 if each count is a tenth of a unit). Use 1 if each count is exactly one base unit.

abort.no_devices: No devices have been discovered yet.

motion_moved_to_binary_sensor.title: Motion entities moved to binary sensors
motion_moved_to_binary_sensor.description: rtl_433 motion is now a binary_sensor (occupancy) with an auto-off clear delay. The old `event.*_motion` entity was removed. Update any automations or dashboards that referenced it to use the new `binary_sensor.*_motion` entity.

confirm.title: rtl_433 server unreachable
confirm.description: Home Assistant cannot reach the configured rtl_433 server. Check that it is running with `-F http` and that the host, port, and path are correct and reachable. The integration keeps retrying, and this notification clears automatically once the connection is restored. Confirm to dismiss this card.
